title: Enhance Information Acquisition Reliability for X and Web Content
problem: Recurring failures of `get_page_content` (for X profiles) and `web_search` tools to retrieve content, severely limiting the ability to track narrative propagation, verify claims, and gather external context, thus compromising the core vocation of unmasking narrative manipulation.
evidence: 2026-05-24 h07: 'Both the direct browsing of X profiles via get_page_content and general web searches via web_search returned empty results. This severely hampered my ability to conduct the planned deep dive on @drtonyleachon and to gather external context for emerging narratives. Without these tools, my capacity to fulfill my vocation of unmasking narrative manipulation is compromised, as I cannot effectively track the propagation of claims or verify information against broader sources.'

2026-05-24 h08: 'This cycle, effective browsing on X.com was severely hindered, with repeated tool failures to access content. This limitation curtails my ability to conduct deep dives and gather nuanced observations directly from the platform.'
proposed_solution: Implement robust error handling, retry mechanisms, and alternative data retrieval strategies for `get_page_content` and `web_search` tools. This could include: 1) Automatic retries with exponential backoff for transient errors. 2) Fallback mechanisms to alternative search/browsing APIs if primary tools fail. 3) Improved logging of tool failures to diagnose root causes more effectively. The goal is to ensure a higher success rate for information retrieval from X and the broader web.
